prior_issues: S2 rank-0 out of SetA band
verdict_summary: Geometry filter PARTIALLY resolved prior issues but introduced new problems. S3 now has 3/4 viable candidates (75% improvement), S2 rank-0 now in band, but S4 remains 100% defective and S1/S3/S3b show extreme PES flatness (<3 meV top-10 spread). Chemistry sanity improved but MLIP energy ranking is unreliable.

evidence.value: 4/4 have physisorption (3/4 CO physisorbed >3.5 Å, 1/4 MeO physisorbed)
4289 fragmented dropped (52% of input), 277 bond-broken dropped
3798 fragmented + 884 C-H broken dropped (59% of input)
5/5 S3 and 5/5 S4 middle ranks structurally intact

concerns: S4 shortlist 100% DEFECTIVE: all 4 candidates have CO or CH3O physisorption. PdO2(110) appears fundamentally incompatible with this reactive-pair setup under MACE-MP relaxation — DFT may yield different binding, but MLIP shortlist provides no useful guidance.

S1/S3/S3b PES EXTREMELY FLAT: top-10 unique within 3 meV (S1), 27 meV (S3), 3 meV (S3b). MACE energy ranking is NOISE at this scale. Cannot trust rank ordering for DFT shortlist selection — geometric diversity is the only discriminant.

S3 rank-0 has MeO physisorbed (Pd-O=3.226 Å) yet 3038 meV ABOVE rank-1,2,3 chemically viable structures. Energy-based ranking is inverted — filter should have used GEOMETRIC criteria (Pd-O < 2.3 Å) not just reactive-distance band.
S1 rank-1 (MACE's lowest-energy) has CO physisorbed (Pd-C=3.677 Å) while rank-0,2 are chemisorbed. Confirms MLIP energy is unreliable even for simple Pd(100).
NO shortlist has rank-0 = lowest energy. Shortlist selection by 'distance-bin representatives' may sacrifice energy ordering for diversity, but producer should have flagged this inversion explicitly.

S1_Pd100.usable_for_DFT: YES with caveat: ignore MACE energies, pick geometrically diverse candidates

S2_PdO101_Pd100.usable_for_DFT: YES — 2 clean candidates (rank-0,1) + good PES spread (215 meV)

S3_PdO100.usable_for_DFT: YES — 3 viable (rank-1,2,3), but MUST validate Pd-O binding in DFT

S3b_PdO100_PdOterm.usable_for_DFT: YES — all clean, but MACE energies meaningless

S4_PdO2_110.usable_for_DFT: NO — all candidates defective. RECOMMEND: skip S4 SetA, or use alternative MLIP (CHGNet/M3GNet), or hand-place reactive pair on known PdO2 sites

filter_resolved: S2 SetA band violation (was rank-0 at 1.9 Å, now all in [2.1, 4.0))
Fragmentation across all surfaces (50-60% of bad structures caught)
Bond-breaking detection (884 C-H breaks in S4)
filter_did_not_resolve: S4 CO physisorption (was issue, still 75% of shortlist)
PES flatness (was <0.5 meV, now 3-27 meV for S1/S3/S3b — still below noise floor)
Energy-ranking inversion (rank-0 still not lowest-energy in any surface)
new_issues_introduced: S3 rank-0 MeO physisorbed (was product-collapse, now different defect)
S1 rank-1 CO physisorbed (not mentioned in prior report)

asks: Revise S4 strategy: either (a) skip SetA on PdO2(110), or (b) try alternative MLIP (CHGNet has better oxide binding), or (c) hand-construct 3-4 reactive pairs on known CUS sites and relax with DFT directly.
For S1/S3/S3b: ignore MLIP energies entirely. Select DFT candidates by GEOMETRIC diversity (d_reactive bins + site-type variety), not by energy rank.
Re-filter S3 shortlist with Pd-O < 2.3 Å criterion: drop rank-0, promote a chemisorbed candidate from unique pool to maintain 4-candidate shortlist.

Validate assumption that 'distance-bin representatives' is correct shortlist strategy. If energy ordering matters, current approach is flawed — shortlists have 50-3000 meV internal spreads while top-10 unique are within 3-27 meV.

Before DFT dispatch: visually render S2 rank-2,3 and S4 all-ranks to confirm CO/MeO positions (Pd-C=3.4-3.7 Å may be bridge-to-different-layer in oxide, not physisorption, but ASE min-distance check cannot distinguish).
recommendation:
CONDITIONAL GO with manual curation:
- S2, S3 (rank-1,2,3), S3b: proceed to DFT Level 1 (vacuum opt) with GEOMETRY-based selection, ignoring MACE energies.
- S1: usable but PES flat — pick all 3 shortlist for diversity.
- S4: STOP. Do not send current shortlist to DFT. Requires alternative approach (see asks).

Geometry filter successfully removed catastrophic failures (fragmentation, product collapse) but cannot fix MLIP's weak oxide chemisorption and flat PES. Phase 2 MLIP role is STRUCTURE GENERATOR, not energy ranker. Treat shortlist as 'plausible geometries' and let DFT re-rank.
